reasoning: The FRED time series tool shows the current T5YIFR at 2.19%, which is fairly low compared to 2024 averages (historically often >2.25%). The FRED linear trend and seasonal models project a roughly 68% chance of the value being higher within a week. While financial drift and mean reversion support upward movement, macroeconomic forecasting horizons inherently carry more noise. Per the strict forecasting instructions to increase uncertainty for more distant horizons, I grade the probabilities down progressively closer to the 0.5 baseline for the distant dates.
